description: P4.6 (TASKS.md): track every version/commit/digest pin this repo maintains by
hand. See docs/renovate.md for the full inventory of what is managed here,
what is deliberately excluded, and why.
STATUS (2026-07-14): Renovate is INSTALLED on this repository and this config
is live. It validates clean against the official tool:
npx --package renovate -- renovate-config-validator renovate.json
Run that before pushing any change to this file -- an invalid config makes
Renovate skip the repo silently rather than fail loudly.
BUT NOTE (2026-07-18): the validator only checks SHAPE, not whether a manager
matches anything. Both bugs fixed today were validator-clean and silent:
1. managerFilePatterns entries are GLOB patterns unless slash-delimited.
`^Makefile$` is a valid glob that matches no file; `/^Makefile$/` is the
regex that works. (This field replaced fileMatch, which was ALWAYS regex --
so the semantics changed under an unchanged-looking value.) All 18 custom
managers were silently extracting zero deps.
2. The kernel custom datasource returned a bare object instead of a one-element
array -- see its own description below.
The only reliable check is a real extraction run, which needs no GitHub token:
npx --package renovate@latest -- renovate --platform=local --dry-run=full
Expect a `regex` entry in the 'Dependency extraction complete' stats with
fileCount 18 / depCount 19. If `regex` is absent, the custom managers are dead
again. Grep the LOG_LEVEL=debug output for `no-result` to catch bug-2-style
datasource failures, which do NOT show up in the extraction stats at all.
automerge is OFF everywhere, deliberately: every pin here is a supply-chain
trust decision (kernel, Buildroot, out-of-tree driver commits, firmware), and
several need a companion hash refreshed by .github/workflows/renovate-hash-sync.yml
before the PR is even buildable. A human reviews each one.

kernelLongterm618.description: kernel.org's machine-readable releases.json, filtered down to the current 6.18.y longterm point release. Deliberately constrained by moniker+prefix here (not just by allowedVersions below) so a schema hiccup upstream can't silently hand back mainline/other-branch versions. NOTE the outer [ ] around the $map: releases.json lists only the LATEST release per branch, so this filter always matches exactly one entry, and JSONata collapses a single-element sequence into a bare object. Renovate's custom datasource requires `releases` to be an ARRAY -- without the explicit array constructor it receives an object, fails schema validation, and reports `no-result` (a silent no-op, not an error). Do not remove the brackets.

kernelMainline.description: kernel.org's releases.json filtered to the CURRENT MAINLINE release -- the line the RT/beta kernel variant tracks (configs/mister_rt.fragment, docs/rt-beta-kernel.md). This is normally a -rc (e.g. 7.2-rc3), which is exactly why it is a separate datasource from kernelLongterm618: the two pins track different upstream lines with different cadence, different provenance, and different review bars, and must never be able to update each other. The outer [ ] is required for the same reason documented above -- releases.json carries exactly one mainline entry, and JSONata would collapse a single-element sequence into a bare object, which Renovate rejects as `no-result` silently.

customManagers.description: Bump the pinned Buildroot release (BUILDROOT_VERSION) in the wrapper Makefile. Deliberately does NOT try to also update the companion BUILDROOT_SHA256 on the next line -- that hash is only legitimate when transcribed from Buildroot's own GPG-signed release manifest (the Makefile's own header comment forbids a locally-computed sha256sum, since that would be circular and would bless a tampered/truncated tarball). A human runs `make buildroot-showsig BUILDROOT_VERSION=<new>` and pastes the signed hash in by hand -- see docs/renovate.md. Until then this PR is RED (buildroot-verify fails closed), which is the intended, safe failure mode.

Bump the pinned 6.18.y kernel (BR2_LINUX_KERNEL_CUSTOM_VERSION_VALUE) EVERYWHERE it appears, constrained to the current longterm line by the custom datasource above. BOTH defconfigs are listed on purpose: configs/mister_kernel_defconfig deliberately copies the main defconfig's kernel/toolchain stanza (see its header and scripts/check-kernel-defconfig-sync.sh), so a manager covering only the main one bumps half the repo and leaves the copy behind -- which is exactly what happened on the 6.18.38 -> 6.18.39 bump and is caught, loudly, by the lockstep check. Because both files carry the same depName, Renovate emits ONE PR touching both. configs/mister_rt.fragment carries the same SYMBOL but is deliberately NOT matched here: it pins the mainline -rc line and has its own manager below. The companion tarball hash (board/mister/de10nano/patches/linux/linux.hash) is NOT updated here -- it comes from kernel.org's own signed sha256sums.asc; see .github/workflows/renovate-hash-sync.yml. CI (build.yml, pull_request-triggered) then proves the carried Linux patches still apply against the bumped source -- see docs/renovate.md and PLAN.md §13.

Bump the RT/beta kernel pin (BR2_LINUX_KERNEL_CUSTOM_VERSION_VALUE in configs/mister_rt.fragment), which tracks the mainline -rc line rather than 6.18.y -- see docs/rt-beta-kernel.md and ADR 0021. Kept as a SEPARATE manager from the 6.18 one above, with its own datasource and its own depName, because the two must never be able to propose each other's version: the fragment carries the identical Kconfig symbol, so a single shared manager would hand the longterm datasource a -rc currentValue (and vice versa). The matchString accepts BOTH an -rc and a plain mainline version (7.2-rc3 and 7.2), because 7.2 final IS the intended destination of this pin -- docs/rt-beta-kernel.md picks 7.2 precisely because PREEMPT_RT landed for ARM in 7.1, so the variant follows that line through its -rc series and out the other side. IMPORTANT -- the companion hash is NEVER auto-refreshed for this pin, unlike the 6.18 one: renovate-hash-sync.yml's kernel step reads its version from the DE10-Nano defconfig and only ever rewrites that pin's own line, so it leaves this one alone in every case. An RT bump PR is therefore EXPECTED to be red at the kernel download ("No hash found for linux-<ver>...", because BR2_DOWNLOAD_FORCE_CHECK_HASHES empties Buildroot's BR_NO_CHECK_HASH_FOR exemption) until a human refreshes it by hand. That is the intended, safe failure mode -- the same shape as the Buildroot hash above. NOTE that the artifact changes shape across the -rc boundary, and so does the provenance a human should demand: while on an -rc, Buildroot fetches a cgit-generated snapshot (linux-<ver>.tar.gz from git.kernel.org) for which kernel.org publishes NO signed manifest, so the value is TOFU per linux.hash's own -rc block; once this pin reaches a stable mainline release it becomes an ordinary linux-<ver>.tar.xz, which IS covered by kernel.org's signed sha256sums.asc and by a maintainer .sign, and should be transcribed from those rather than TOFU-pinned.
RTL8812AU driver commit pin (morrownr/8812au-20210820, main branch). Hash: package/rtl8812au/rtl8812au.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
RTL8814AU driver commit pin (morrownr/8814au, main branch). Hash: package/rtl8814au-morrownr/rtl8814au-morrownr.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
RTL8821AU driver commit pin (morrownr/8821au-20210708, main branch). Hash: package/rtl8821au-morrownr/rtl8821au-morrownr.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
RTL8821CU driver commit pin (morrownr/8821cu-20210916, main branch). Hash: package/rtl8821cu-morrownr/rtl8821cu-morrownr.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
RTL8188FU driver commit pin (kelebek333/rtl8188fu, master branch). Hash: package/rtl8188fu/rtl8188fu.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.

RTL8188EU driver commit pin (aircrack-ng/rtl8188eus). NOTE: this repo's default branch is literally named 'v5.3.9' (verified via git ls-remote --symref at authoring time, not assumed) -- update currentValueTemplate if aircrack-ng ever renames it, or this manager will quietly stop finding new commits. Hash: package/rtl8188eu-aircrack-ng/rtl8188eu-aircrack-ng.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
RTL88X2BU driver commit pin (morrownr/88x2bu-20210702, main branch). Hash: package/rtl88x2bu/rtl88x2bu.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
xone (Xbox GIP) driver commit pin (dlundqvist/xone, master branch). Hash: package/xone/xone.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.
MidiLink_MiSTer commit pin (MiSTer-devel/MidiLink_MiSTer, master branch). Hash: package/midilink/midilink.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.

munt MT-32 emulator tag pin. Non-standard tag format (munt_MAJOR_MINOR_PATCH) needs a regex versioning scheme so Renovate orders releases correctly instead of treating the whole string as an opaque blob. Hash: package/munt/munt.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.

BCM20702 Bluetooth firmware COMMIT pin (winterheart/broadcom-bt-firmware, master branch). Was a github-tags/loose TAG pin until 2026-07-19; changed for two independent reasons. (1) Upstream stopped tagging: the newest tag v12.0.1.1105_p4 is from 2022-10-10, while master is still active (three firmware commits on 2026-07-08), so a tag pin could no longer see real updates. (2) The tag scheme actively misled the ordering: the trailing _pN is a patch level ON TOP of the base version, so v12.0.1.1105_p4 is NEWER than v12.0.1.1105 -- but under `loose` the bare tag sorts higher, and Renovate kept proposing v12.0.1.1105 (2020-05-02) as an upgrade, i.e. a 2.5-year DOWNGRADE. A commit pin removes the ordering question entirely instead of trying to teach `loose` a bespoke scheme, and matches how the 9 other untagged upstreams here are pinned. Safe because the package installs exactly ONE file and the .mk's inline sha256sum of that .hcd fails the build CLOSED if it ever changes -- the switch itself was a verified no-op (the .hcd is byte-identical at the old tag and at master). Hash: package/bcm20702-firmware/bcm20702-firmware.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml's generic loop.

libchdr commit pin (rtissera/libchdr, master branch) -- the CHD read library for the Main_MiSTer shared-lib refactor; pinned to a branch-head commit, not the stale v0.3.0 tag (see libchdr.mk's header for why). Hash: package/libchdr/libchdr.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml's generic loop (standard $(call github,...) tarball).

7-Zip LZMA SDK tag pin (ip7z/7zip) for the Main_MiSTer shared-lib refactor. Only the LZMA_SDK_VERSION line needs managing -- LZMA_SDK_SOURCE derives from it via $(subst) in the .mk. Hash: package/lzma-sdk/lzma-sdk.hash, refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml's BESPOKE lzma-sdk step (release-asset URL with a dots-stripped filename, does not fit the generic $(call github,...) loop).

Update_All_MiSTer launcher pinned into the sdcard payload (theypsilon/Update_All_MiSTer, master branch). Tracks the branch HEAD and bumps the 40-char commit; the SHA-256/size companions (PINNED_UPDATE_ALL_SHA256/SIZE in the same file) are refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.

wifi.sh pinned into the sdcard payload (MiSTer-devel/Scripts_MiSTer other_authors/wifi.sh, master branch). Tracks the branch HEAD and bumps the 40-char commit; the SHA-256/size companions (PINNED_WIFI_SH_SHA256/SIZE in the same file) are refreshed by renovate-hash-sync.yml.

_Console cores snapshot commit for the opt-in sdcard-full.img (MiSTer-devel/Distribution_MiSTer, main branch). Commit-only pin -- individual cores are fetched by content, not hash-verified (see scripts/fetch-sdcard-payload.sh), so there is no companion hash to sync.

packageRules.description: Constrain the Buildroot pin to the 2026.05.x line -- do not let Renovate jump to a new Buildroot major/minor; P1.1's whole toolchain/config pin assumes 2026.05. (2026.05 is NOT an LTS; 2025.02 was the last LTS and 2027.02 is expected to be the next -- see docs. This is the interim non-LTS line until 2027.02 ships.)

Constrain the kernel pin to the 6.18.y longterm line (defense in depth -- the custom datasource above already filters to moniker=longterm and 6.18.*). A kernel MINOR bump is out of scope for routine automation and must not be silently allowed here.

RT/beta kernel pin (configs/mister_rt.fragment), tracking mainline -rc. Constrained to the 7.x line as defense in depth, mirroring the 6.18 rule above: a jump to a new MAJOR is a deliberate decision about what the beta variant tracks, not routine automation. Labeled distinctly from kernel-pin so a reviewer is never in doubt about WHICH kernel a PR moves -- the two carry the identical Kconfig symbol and otherwise look alike in a diff. needs-manual-hash is not decorative: this PR cannot go green until a human writes the new hash into linux.hash by hand. The hash-sync workflow never touches this line -- and how the value should be obtained depends on which side of the -rc boundary the pin is on (TOFU for a cgit -rc snapshot, kernel.org's signed manifest for a stable release). See the manager description. needs-manual-version-check is carried for the same reason bcm20702-firmware is: `loose` versioning's ordering of a -rcN suffix is not something to trust blindly.
Driver/firmware commit and tag pins -- label distinctly so a reviewer knows these change out-of-tree kernel-module source, not just a build tool version.

Main_MiSTer shared-library pins (libchdr commit, LZMA SDK tag) -- distinct label per the established per-pin-class convention, so a reviewer knows these change a userspace library ABI Main_MiSTer links against (liblzma-sdk's SONAME is the full version, so an SDK bump IS an ABI event by design), not kernel-module source or a build tool.

SD-card payload pins (update_all.sh, wifi.sh, and the _Console cores snapshot fetched by scripts/fetch-sdcard-payload.sh). Label distinctly so a reviewer knows these change what a freshly-flashed sdcard.img installs, not a build tool or kernel-module source.
Keep CI container image digests (ubuntu:26.04@sha256:... in build.yml/release.yml/reproducibility.yml's `container:` blocks) pinned and updated in place.

GitHub Actions SHA-pinned `uses:` lines (with a trailing # vX.Y.Z comment) across build/release/reproducibility/publish-db workflows -- handled by Renovate's built-in github-actions manager (digest update, comment kept in sync). No custom manager needed; this rule just labels the PRs.
